name: Dalton-Whitfield Community Dev. Corp.
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor in Northwest Georgia offering HUD-certified counseling, permanent supportive housing, and health support housing programs for low-income families.
description_long:
Dalton-Whitfield Community Development Corporation (DWCDC) is a non-profit organization that has served Northwest Georgia residents for over 20 years. The organization focuses exclusively on housing stability and community support, working one household at a time to help families access safe, affordable housing and long-term stability. DWCDC operates within the Housing Stability Coalition, a network of nonprofits, schools, faith groups, and legal aid organizations unified around strengthening housing stability in Dalton.

DWCDC offers three primary service lines: (1) Permanent Supportive Housing for formerly homeless individuals with disabilities, providing safe and affordable rentals; (2) Health Support Housing for people with qualifying health conditions; and (3) HUD-certified housing counseling for first-time homebuyers, renters, and current homeowners facing financial difficulty or foreclosure. Their counseling services are delivered by trained professionals including Marlene Alfaro, their dedicated Housing Counselor. The organization also employs specialists in permanent supportive housing (Karen Sutton) and health support housing (Mayvit Corbin), with Reed Fincher serving as Executive Director.

What distinguishes DWCDC is its deep specialization in housing stability for vulnerable populations—particularly the formerly homeless, disabled, and those with health conditions—rather than offering broad consumer finance services. Their HUD-certification provides credibility and adherence to federal housing counseling standards. The organization's two-decade track record and structured partnerships through the Housing Stability Coalition demonstrate institutional stability and community trust. They explicitly position themselves as a crisis intervention resource for people experiencing acute housing emergencies.

DWCDC is best suited for consumers facing homelessness, housing instability, or seeking first-time homebuyer education in the Dalton, Georgia area. Those with health conditions or disabilities seeking housing assistance will find targeted support. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services appear localized to Northwest Georgia. Additionally, unlike personal finance companies, DWCDC does not provide loans, credit repair, or debt management; they are exclusively a counseling and housing placement organization.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselor ensures compliance with federal standards for housing counseling
Specializes in Permanent Supportive Housing for formerly homeless individuals with disabilities
Over 20 years of established service history in Northwest Georgia community
Part of Housing Stability Coalition providing coordinated multi-sector support (nonprofits, schools, faith groups, legal aid)
Offers Health Support Housing for people with qualifying health conditions—targeted assistance not widely available
Free service model as non-profit means no predatory fees or hidden costs
Team includes dedicated specialists for different housing programs (supportive housing, health support, counseling)
cons: Geographic limitation—services appear restricted to Northwest Georgia (Dalton area)
No online application or intake process visible; requires direct contact to access services
Does not provide financial products (loans, credit repair, debt management)—counseling only
No pricing, availability, or waitlist information published on website
Limited online presence and digital engagement tools compared to larger organizations
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Northwest Georgia seeking HUD-approved counseling
Formerly homeless individuals with disabilities needing permanent supportive housing
People with health conditions seeking housing assistance and stability support
Renters and homeowners facing foreclosure or financial difficulty requiring counseling
diagnosis: DWCDC is best for Northwest Georgia residents experiencing housing crises or seeking first-time homebuyer counseling from a HUD-certified, non-profit source. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to the Dalton area and complete absence of financial products or credit services—this is purely a housing counseling and placement organization, not a consumer finance company.
services: Housing crisis intervention and resource connection
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
